seoTitle: Windy Harbour vs Winthrop — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Windy Harbour and Winthrop. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Windy Harbour vs Winthrop: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Windy Harbour and Winthrop.
heading: Windy Harbour vs Winthrop — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Windy Harbour, Western Australia and Winthrop, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Windy Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Winthrop (37/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Windy Harbour or Winthrop safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Windy Harbour has a flood risk score of 39/100, while Winthrop scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Windy Harbour scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Winthrop scores 21/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Windy Harbour or Winthrop?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Windy Harbour has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.05% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
